title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Jong-hindajiman! I'm going to show you my study routine. I'll teach you English expressions related to studying. I have an upcoming test, so I'll be hitting the books and studying hard all day today. I've been taking Korean language classes and I've had almost perfect attendance, but to be honest, I've been slacking off on my homework lately. So today, I have to cram and catch up on my assignments. Let's study together. I'll be studying Korean, but you can practice your English by watching this video. So grab your notebook, get comfy, and let's get started.

First things first, I'm going to set up my desk for studying. I like having a space that's clean and simple, so I'm going to clear everything off of my desk. Thank you. I just like to keep a notebook and pen and a water bottle on my desk. I will also be using my computer, so I'm going to put on my blue light glasses. Well, do I look nerdy? I'm going to start with vocabulary because it's what I struggle with the most. I have a terrible memory and forget things so easily. So I use an app called Quizlet to help me memorize key vocabulary. While making flashcards, I like to listen to the pronunciation and practice saying the words out loud so that I can get used to saying them naturally.

I'm going to do some homework in my workbook. As I go through my workbook, I like to highlight or underline words that I don't know so that I can look them up later. Hey, cameraman, what do you call this in English?

It's kind of a hard word, mechanical pencil. To help me stay focused, I use a Pomodoro timer and study in intervals. So I'll study for 25 minutes straight and then I'll take a quick five minute break. It helps me be productive and stay on track. I usually just find one on YouTube. Okay, my timer is set and I'm going to put my phone to the side so I don't get distracted.
The timer just went off so it's time for a brain break. Taking short breaks is very important so you don't burn out. I'm going to use my break to just relax and scroll on my phone.

Now it's time for my least favorite part of studying grammar. As I study I take notes in my notebook, writing things down and making my own example sentences helps me understand better. These days I've been practicing the grammar point. I always get these mixed up and it's pretty frustrating but the more I practice the quicker I'll get the hang of it. This time I'm going to listen to some music while I study. I usually listen to heavy metal while studying grammar to get rid of the rage inside. Just kidding. I listen to instrumental music that is calming like lofi or acoustic covers. Heavy metal. I always love a good Taylor Swift instrumental playlist.

So grab your notebook, get comfy, and let's get started.

title: should (advice)
examples: Oh, shouldn't doubt myself.
Why are you making fun of me? You should be supporting me!
